declared.title: State of Internet Freedom in Africa 2025: Navigating the Implications of AI on Digital Democracy in Kenya
declared.selection_rationale: Single-country companion report to the continent-wide 2025 SIFA report already in this corpus (cipesa-africa-2025-sifa-ai) -- not a duplicate, same pattern already confirmed for the Uganda companion. Confirmed by downloading and extracting the full PDF (32 pages, 12,322 words, all pages text-bearing, no abridgment language found): by Francis Monyango, published September 2025. Coverage-contribution value: specific, dated findings absent elsewhere in this corpus's Kenya entries -- the June 25, 2024 nationwide internet disruption during the #RejectFinanceBill2024 protests (Cloudflare/NetBlocks traffic analysis, spillover into Uganda/Rwanda/Burundi); KNCHR's December 26, 2024 statement documenting 82 enforced disappearances June-December 2024 targeting online activists and AI-meme creators; the 2025 Worldcoin biometric-iris-scan controversy later ruled unlawful by Kenya's High Court; the March 6, 2026 Court of Appeal ruling striking down the 2018 Computer Misuse and Cybercrimes Act's false-publication provisions as unconstitutional; and named surveillance infrastructure (the National Police Service's ICCS with NeoFace Watch facial recognition across Nairobi/Mombasa). Meaningfully deeper than the continent report's compressed per-country Kenya treatment.
